improvement_roadmap.action: Enhance Path #1 realism by adding intermediate hops
improvement_roadmap.verification_method: Verify Path #1 has minimum 2-3 hops representing realistic attack progression through network layers, authentication, and application logic
improvement_roadmap.expected_outcome: More realistic attack path modeling that reflects actual network architecture

improvement_roadmap.action: Replace CODE SIGNING with web-specific controls
improvement_roadmap.verification_method: Confirm control list includes INPUT VALIDATION, SQL injection prevention, or XSS protection instead of or alongside CODE SIGNING
improvement_roadmap.expected_outcome: Controls better aligned with web application threat landscape

improvement_roadmap.verification_method: Review after.mmd for logical grouping of controls and clear visual separation between original and new components
improvement_roadmap.expected_outcome: Improved diagram readability while maintaining completeness

gaps.description: Validation system flags 12 'generic technique' issues but these appear to be validation system limitations rather than actual MITRE mapping errors
gaps.recommendation: Review validation system logic for generic technique handling or provide more specific technique context

gaps.description: CODE SIGNING control may be less relevant for web application context compared to INPUT VALIDATION or XSS protection
gaps.recommendation: Consider replacing or supplementing CODE SIGNING with web-specific controls like Content Security Policy or SQL injection prevention

gaps.description: Architect roadmap verification methods lack specific metrics and success criteria
gaps.recommendation: Add measurable verification criteria like 'Path #1 should have minimum 3 distinct network/authentication/application hops' and specific point calculation methodology

strengths: All MITRE ATT&CK mappings are technically valid using proper hybrid defense-in-depth approach
Comprehensive coverage of all 6 RAPIDS categories with risk-appropriate control allocation
Excellent technique coverage with 11 unique techniques across 3 attack paths
Strong defense-in-depth strategy with 8-12 controls per critical attack path
Realistic risk reduction calculation (81.4% reduction) supported by comprehensive control coverage
All artifacts present and complete including technical diagrams and executive summaries

red_team.rating: MEDIUM = moderately difficult to exploit = MODERATE defense

exploit_mitigation_roadmap.target_rating: LOW = difficult to exploit = GOOD defense
exploit_mitigation_roadmap.attacker_impact: Forces use of advanced evasion techniques, requires APT-level tradecraft for lateral movement
exploit_mitigation_roadmap.tradeoffs: Moderate operational overhead for tuning IDS rules, some network latency

exploit_mitigation_roadmap.target_rating: LOW = very difficult to exploit = STRONG defense
exploit_mitigation_roadmap.attacker_impact: Requires nation-state level capabilities, custom zero-day exploits, and sophisticated social engineering
exploit_mitigation_roadmap.tradeoffs: High user friction, significant operational complexity, requires specialized security team

exploit_mitigation_roadmap.target_rating: MINIMAL = nearly impossible to exploit = EXCELLENT defense
exploit_mitigation_roadmap.attacker_impact: Requires insider access combined with nation-state resources and physical compromise
exploit_mitigation_roadmap.tradeoffs: System becomes nearly unusable for business operations, extreme operational overhead
